items.title: El uso de gas lacrimógeno y la posible judicialización de manifestantes pone en riesgo la protesta ciudadana

items.title: Venezuela: Censorship exacerbates earthquake crisis, putting more lives at risk
items.summary: The devastation caused by the earthquakes in Venezuela on 24 June has put a spotlight on the years of censorship and crackdowns on journalism that have severely weakened the country’s news landscape. items.title: Board votes to affirm CPJ’s existing definition of who is a journalist
items.summary: The Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ) Board of Directors voted on Wednesday to affirm the organization’s existing definition of who is a journalist and rejected claims it planned to change its definition to exclude particular groups. “CPJ frequently considers its definition of a journalist,...

items.title: Colombia: Election of Abelardo de la Espriella, known for targeting journalists with legal action, raises press freedom concerns amid persistent violence
items.summary: Following the election of Abelardo de la Espriella as president of Colombia, Reporters Without Borders (RSF) is concerned about the future of press freedom in the country. items.title: Mexico: A journalist murdered Veracruz just as country opens 2026 World Cup
items.summary: Reporters Without Borders (RSF) condemns the murder of journalist Luis Ángel López Valdez, killed on 11 June in Poza Rica, in the northern part of the state of Veracruz, on the very day that Mexico was inaugurating the 2026 FIFA World Cup. items.title: Ante el desistimiento del Ministerio Público en el caso contra periodistas y columnistas de elPeriódico
items.summary: La Red Rompe el Miedo Guatemala (RRMG) recibe con satisfacción la decisión del Ministerio Público de desistir de las acciones promovidas contra periodistas y columnistas de elPeriódico, por publicaciones realizadas en el ejercicio de su labor informativa. items.summary: Reporters Without Borders (RSF) condemns the murder of Colombian journalist Cristian Hernando Herrera Nariño, who was shot dead on Saturday, 6 June in Cúcuta, a city on the border with Venezuela. items.title: El trauma: una guía rápida para comprender un fenómeno complejo
items.summary: En el ejercicio periodístico, el trauma aparece con frecuencia en las historias que se investigan, se cubren y se narran. Sin embargo, comprenderlo requiere ir más allá del hecho doloroso, disruptivo o aparentemente excepcional. También implica atender la forma en que cada persona vive esa experi...

items.title: RSF condemns attacks and threats against The Intercept Brasil after its investigation involving presidential candidate Flávio Bolsonaro
items.summary: Reporters Without Borders (RSF) condemns the doxxing and severe cyber harassment targeting the staff of investigative news site The Intercept Brasil following recent investigations into Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, a leading candidate in Brazil’s October presidential election. items.title: Mexico: RSF, co-organiser of the Breach/Valdez Prize for Journalism and Human Rights, congratulates the eighth edition’s winners

items.title: Colombian president ends term with projects for safeguarding press freedom still unfinished

items.summary: Reporters Without Borders (RSF) condemns the forced closure of Radio Stereo Romance, one of the most renowned radio stations in southern Nicaragua, silenced on 8 May 2026. items.title: Investigation into Guatemalan journalist’s murder must consider link to his work
items.summary: Reporters Without Borders (RSF) calls on Guatemala’s public prosecutor's office and the specialised prosecutor's office for crimes against journalists to conduct a swift, thorough and transparent investigation into journalist Carlos Humberto Cal Ical’s murder in the north of the country on 26 Apr...

items.title: Unprecedented proceeding against Google opens way to fair compensation for journalism in Brazil
items.summary: For the first time in Brazil, the competition authority has opened a proceeding against a tech giant to investigate the harm it caused to journalism. It has been launched into Google News and Google’s search engine, whose features utilise generative artificial intelligence (AI). Reporters Without...

items.title: Consejos de autocuidado para periodistas: una pausa también es defender el oficio
items.summary: El trabajo periodístico exige atención constante, capacidad de reacción, jornadas intensas y, muchas veces, exposición a situaciones complejas. El autocuidado es una práctica necesaria para sostener la capacidad de seguir ejerciendo el oficio. Desde la Red Centroamericana de Periodistas compartim...
